original_abstract: This research investigates the morphological characteristics of Arabic verbs within Surah An-Naba’ in the 30th Juz of the Al-Qur'an, focusing specifically on *Fi’il Madhi* (past tense) and *Fi’il Mudhori* (present/future tense). Adopting a qualitative library research methodology, the study utilizes linguistic approaches from the fields of *Nahwu* (syntax) and *Shorof* (morphology) to identify verb patterns (*wazan*) and their associated pronouns (*dhomir*). The primary objective is to categorize these linguistic features to provide an accessible learning framework for students and memorizers who frequently begin their Al-Qur'an studies with this chapter. Findings reveal a total of 16 distinct verb instances categorized as *Fi’il Mudhari*, showcasing a variety of morphological structures and pronominal attachments. By detailing the stems, roots, and patterns of these verbs, the paper aims to bridge the gap between rote memorization and grammatical comprehension, ultimately facilitating a deeper technical and semantic understanding of the Quranic text for learners in Islamic educational institutions.
humanized_abstract: The study examines the morphology of the Arabic verbs that are found in Surah An-Naba’ located in the 30th Juz of the Al-Qur'an. The study specifically focused on the *Fi’il Madhi* and *Fi’il Mudhori* that are found in the whole chapter. It makes use of the qualitative library research methodology and it applied some linguistic rules and principles that were derived from the aspects of *Nahwu* and *Shorof* to the structure of the Arabic verbs found in Surah An-Naba’ in order to identify the different patterns of the verbs *wazan* and the pronouns *dhomir* that are attached to them. In this way, it classifies and analyses the Arabic verbs in Surah An-Naba’ in order to show in a clear manner possible patterns of the Arabic verbs that are used in Surah An-Naba’ that students and memorizers can make use of when they start their journey with the Al-Qur'an as it is a habit that Surah An-Naba’ is always included in the 30th Juz of the Al-Qur'an. In this study, it is found out that there are 16 instances of the *Fi’il Mudhari* that are used in Surah An-Naba’. And it identified and explained the different morphological structures of the Arabic verbs and the pronouns that are attached to them in order to show the stems, roots and patterns of the Arabic verbs and therefore make it easy for the students and memorizers of the Quran to understand it in a more technical way as well as in a semantic way so that it can help them to increase their knowledge of the Quran. Consequently, this study recommended that the Islamic educational institutions should include this aspect in their curriculum so as to develop the students and memorizers knowledge of the Quran.
